history.text: Early medieval Bosnia anchors the start of the documented local timeline.
Banate of Bosnia ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
The Kingdom of Bosnia emerged, giving the area a defined monarchical state structure.
Ottoman conquest changed control of the territory.
Austro-Hungarian conquest and occupation placed the territory under outside administration.
Austro-Hungarian annexation changed control of the territory.
Absorbed into the Kingdom of Yugoslavia ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
ZAVNOBiH appears in Bosnia and Herzegovina's historical record.

worthSeeing.title: Stari Most Mostar:
worthSeeing.text: UNESCO bridge symbolising reconciliation spanning the Neretva river.

worthSeeing.title: Sarajevo History:
worthSeeing.text: The city that sparked WWI, with Ottoman bazaar, Austro-Hungarian avenues and siege memorials.

worthSeeing.title: Una River:
worthSeeing.text: Crystal-clear rapids and waterfalls in a pristine national park.
